August 3, 1981 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento CA 95814 
Attn: 
Alice Hughes 
a\ 
The City of Petaluma held its biennial City Council and School Board election on 
June 2, 1981. 
In connection with that election, there is some conflict and 
question regarding filing Forms 400 series. 
Please advise the correct procedure 
relating to situations listed below: 
1. 
As a charter city, the school board election is governed by our election. 
The Education Code recognizes this and even states the entire school district 
is considered part of the City when charters cite school board elections in 
connection with municipal elections. 
Now, who is the filing officer in those circumstances, the City or the County? 
But, wait. 
The situation becomes more awkward. 
2. 
There are different provisions for filing times between June 2 elections and 
elections held at any other time. 
Page 6 of the 1981 Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure states the 
1981 filing schedule for June 2 elections to be on activity through June 30 
should be reported at a July 31 deadline. 
That is 28 days after election 
the candidates close their books and report that information 59 days after 
election. 
Page 8 of the same manual says candidates close their books 58 days after 
election and make the reporting 65 days after the election. 
Now, there is a 30 day difference in the time the candidates are required 
to close their books and a 6 day difference in the filing deadline date. 


Fair Political Practices Commission 
August 3, 1981 
-2-
The County Clerk's office has required the school board candidates to file with 
the County by the July 31 deadline. 
I, as Petaluma City Clerk, have requested 
all the candidates to file with me giving them the benefit of the 58 day closing 
date for accounting purposes and the 65 day deadline date for submitting their 
Forms 400 series. 
If the County is the filing agency, that is fine with me. 
Please advise which 
Clerk should receive the school board filings for this Charter City, Petaluma. 
Now for the filing date discrepancies. It is hoped that all city elections 
would be governed by the 58 day closing date after election and 65 day filing 
deadline after election rather than that which is listed for June 2 and November 3 
elections. 
Please advise your interpretation of filing regulations on this 
matter. 
Thank you for your time. 
Sincerely y~rs, 
Bernard 
City 
cc: 
County Clerk 
Ci ty Attorney 
Acting City Manager

sections.question_synthetic: For a charter city's school board election held in conjunction with a municipal election, should Form 400 series filings be submitted to the City Clerk or County Clerk? Additionally, what are the correct filing deadlines and closing dates for candidates—are they governed by the 28/59-day schedule for June 2 elections or the 58/65-day schedule for other elections?
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. OCR quality is poor with significant character corruption throughout the document, making some sections difficult to parse. No conclusion or short answer section is present. The question section is clearly identifiable as the body of the letter from the City Clerk requesting guidance.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from the Petaluma City Clerk to the FPPC dated August 3, 1981, asking for clarification on Form 400 filing procedures and deadlines following a June 2, 1981 charter city election that included school board candidates.
